scenarios.description: load() with default settings and empty .env yields empty Dotenv without setting any system properties (branch systemProperties=false, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Create an empty .env file in a temporary directory; DotenvBuilder uses that directory; no mocking
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Dotenv.entriesInFile() is empty; no new System.getProperty() entries for dotenv keys

scenarios.description: load() with default settings and one entry sets that single entry as system property and returns it (branch systemProperties=false, loop-1)
scenarios.precondition: Create a .env file containing KEY=VALUE; no mocking

scenarios.description: load() with default settings and multiple entries sets all entries as system properties and returns full set (branch systemProperties=false, loop-N)
scenarios.precondition: Create a .env file with three distinct key-value lines; no mocking
scenarios.expectedResult: returned entries contain exactly those three keys with their values; each System.getProperty(key) equals its value
pseudocode: assertSetEquals(dotenv.entries(), Set.of(new DotenvEntry("A","1"), new DotenvEntry("B","2"), new DotenvEntry("C","3")));

scenarios.description: load() with systemProperties enabled skips setting any system properties but returns all entries (branch systemProperties=true, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Create a .env file containing X=Y; no mocking
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Dotenv.get("X") equals "Y"; System.getProperty("X") remains null
pseudocode: TEST TC04 load with systemProperties skips applying

scenarios.description: load() throws DotenvException when parser.parse() fails (exception path)
scenarios.precondition: Use Mockito to mock DotenvParser.parse() to throw DotenvException because constructor injection can't be replaced (mocking necessary)
scenarios.expectedResult: throws DotenvException; no System properties changed
